synthesis:
On the full 40-lesson sweep, the headline of the verb-system pilot holds and sharpens: Kochergina's *factual accuracy is high* — the large majority of registered assertions are TRUE against the DCS-2021 corpus and Whitney 1889 — and her failures are almost never errors of fact but of *calibration*. Her presentation principle is the paradigm-completeness tradition of the Petersburg/Soviet school: give every form its rule and its exceptions, ordered by the grammatical system, and trust the learner to drill the tables. That principle is well-suited to accuracy and to reading the classical language, and it produces the many clean TRUE/JUSTIFIED anchors here (neuter N=A syncretism HK-13, one-final-consonant phonotactics HK-31, pronominal sarvam HK-30, the reduplication and passive-alignment rules).

Two systematic failure modes recur across the whole book. **(1) Universality overreach.** The beginner-facing rule is stated with an absolute quantifier — "всегда", "только", "по единому правилу" — where a well-known exception class exists. The sharpest case is HK-16 ("основы на -ī и -ū всегда женского рода"), which is simply FALSE: masculine ī/ū-stems (rathī, senānī, sudhī, monosyllabic bhū-) are well attested; she means the derived nadī/vadhū class. The same overreach drives HK-10 (accentless finite verb "никогда" — but it IS accented in a subordinate clause, Whitney §168), HK-14/HK-34 (a-/compound gender "always" the final/derived value — bahuvrīhi gender tracks the referent, contradicted in her own lesson), HK-25 (simple perfect "от всех односложных" — vid/ās/long-vowel roots take periphrastic), HK-38 (root aorist "от некоторых -ā и bhū" — the attested root-aorist set is much wider), and HK-40 (double-preverb ordering "всегда"). **(2) Frequency-blindness.** The tradition orders material by systematic regularity, not by how often a form is actually met, so a canonical "base rule" is foregrounded over the statistically dominant realisation of it. HK-4 (future -syá taught as the single rule while the seṭ -iṣya is 56.8% of forms) is the template; HK-15 (imperfect "обычно" the narrative past — stratum-dependent), HK-19 (class-IX "большинство носовой+согласная" — doubtful), HK-26, HK-32 all repeat it.

A third, smaller bucket is genuinely **UNTESTABLE**: the precise percentages Kochergina asserts without a stated count base — HK-41 (prefixed-verb 60/30/<10% semantic split) and HK-42 (a- privative ≥1/3 of prefixed nouns, ~80% abstract). Directionally plausible, but not reproducible without her coding scheme; flagged rather than assumed. Where the corpus *can* adjudicate a frequency claim, it usually vindicates her: the conditional (HK-23, 0.03%), the middle precative (HK-39, 0.03%) and the 2nd-person imperative dominance (HK-17, 63%) all land where she says.

Recommendation for the reading-site overlay: keep Kochergina's text intact (it is accurate) and badge the two failure modes — the FALSE/OVERSTATED universals with the counter-class, and the frequency-hidden claims with the corpus number — so the learner sees where an absolute rule has an escape hatch and where a "variant" is in fact the norm. The register generalises: the two axes are worth running on any grammar whose ordering predates corpus statistics (roadmap spine S1/S2).

claims.claim_ru: Формы аориста образуются лишь от части корней. В ведийском языке аорист мог употребляться примерно от половины глагольных корней. Постепенно количество корней, употребляемых в формах аориста, уменьшается, и в санскрите аорист встречается редко.
claims.number: 690 distinct aorist forms; 2,452 aorist tokens = 0.31% of 781,618 verbal tokens (DCS-2021), vs present 157,003 (20.1%)
claims.ref: Whitney §824 (aorist recessive in classical); DCS verify_claims_dcs.py HK-1
claims.note: A model presentation: Kochergina states *which* part (≈half in Vedic, receding to rare in Classical) and *why* (diachronic loss), matching the corpus (0.31% of verbal tokens). The hedge "лишь от части" is if anything an understatement of how marginal the aorist is in the classical corpus. No frequency hidden.

claims.claim_ru: При склонении неопределенных местоимений изменяется только первая их часть, т.е. вопросительное местоимение; неизменяемые частицы (api, ca, cana, cid) прибавляются к падежной форме с соблюдением правил внешних сандхи.
claims.number: systemic: attested paradigm kaścid / kiṃcid / kenacid / kasmiṃścit — k-stem inflects, particle constant; joined by external sandhi (kaḥ+cid→kaścid, kim+cid→kiṃcid)
claims.ref: Whitney §507 (interrogative + ca/caná/cid/ápi → indefinite); Kochergina's own paradigm mdx 6256-6258
claims.note: Correct and stated at the right altitude. The particle is genuinely invariant and the junction is external sandhi; MG's existing kenacid counts in DCS confirm the attested paradigm. Nothing overstated.

claims.claim_ru: Пассивное причастие будущего времени — причастие долженствования (participium necessitatis, pn.) — образуется прибавлением к корню суффиксов -ya, -(i)tavya или -anīya с одинаковым грамматическим значением (пассивность + модальность долженствования + будущее время).
claims.number: 1,799 distinct gerundive forms; 23,942 tokens = 3.06% of verbal (DCS Fut.Pass.Part.). Suffix split by type: -ya 1046 / -tavya 523 / -anīya 230. cf PPP 233,080 tokens (29.82%)
claims.note: Formation description and the three-suffix inventory are accurate. Answering MG's "насколько распространено": the gerundive is a real but minor category (3.06% of verbal tokens), and the simple -ya type outnumbers -tavya and -anīya together among distinct forms. Kochergina lists the suffixes without ranking them; a frequency note (-ya dominant) would improve it but nothing is misstated.

claims.claim_ru: Основа будущего времени образуется от ВСЕХ глаголов по единому правилу — прибавлением к корню ударяемого суффикса -syá.
claims.number: future is productive (18,004 finite fut. tokens, 2,618 distinct forms) but NOT one rule: three conditioned suffixes -sya/-ṣya/-iṣya, and the seṭ form -iṣya is the majority (56.8% of distinct forms) — see HK-4b
claims.ref: Whitney §931-935 (s-future, seṭ/aniṭ -iṣya vs -sya); DCS verify_claims_dcs.py HK-4
claims.note: "От всех глаголов" is defensible as a productivity/paradigm claim (the future is regularly formable). But "по единому правилу -syá" is overstated: the actual conditioning is the seṭ/aniṭ lexical class (whether a root takes the connecting -i-), not a single suffix. Presenting -syá as THE rule and -ṣya/-iṣya as mere "варианты" inverts corpus reality — see HK-4b. Ties to roadmap spine S2 (seṭ/aniṭ).

claims.claim_ru: Корни на согласные могут иметь варианты этого суффикса -ṣya или -iṣya. Суффикс -sya прибавляется к корням на -ā, -ṣya — к корням на другие гласные, -iṣya — к корням на дифтонги или на -ar.
claims.number: distinct future forms (DCS-2021): -iṣya 1,487 (56.8%) · -sya 652 (24.9%) · -ṣya 479 (18.3%). The seṭ form -iṣya is the single most common, not a marginal variant.
claims.ref: Whitney §934-935 (union-vowel i in the s-future); DCS verify_claims_dcs.py HK-4
claims.note: The three suffixes exist and the -ā→-sya rule holds, so the surface facts are right. But calling -ṣya/-iṣya "варианты" of a base -sya buries the corpus fact that -iṣya (seṭ) is the MAJORITY outcome (56.8%). The conditioning Kochergina gives is phonological (root-final sound), whereas the real predictor is the seṭ/aniṭ lexical class — a learner drilled on "-sya is the rule" will mis-predict the most common case.
claims.mg_footnote: В корпусе DCS-2021 «вариант» -iṣya (seṭ) — самая частая форма будущего: 56,8% различных форм (1 487), против -sya 24,9% (652) и -ṣya 18,3% (479). То, что подано как исключение, на деле — норма.

claims.claim_ru: II тип [аориста] — тематический простой аорист. Самый распространенный в санскрите тип аориста. Обычно образуется от корней на согласную.
claims.number: DCS timws.csv tags aorist only as Act/Med (codes 10-13, 2,452 tokens), not by the 7 formation types — per-type split needs finer morphology than the 2021 export carries
claims.ref: Whitney §846-847 (a-aorist common, from roots in consonants)
claims.note: Consistent with Whitney's account, but the DCS-2021 export does not tag aorists by formation type, so the 'самый распространенный' ranking cannot be confirmed against the corpus here. Flagged UNTESTABLE rather than assumed. Revisit if the DCS-2026 CoNLL-U import adds sub-type tags.

claims.claim_ru: Пассивные причастия настоящего времени склоняются как основы на -a мужского и среднего рода; причастия женского рода образуются удлинением конечной -a и склоняются как основы на -ā.
claims.number: systemic: -māna/-yamāna participles are thematic -a stems, f. -ā — standard thematic declension
claims.note: Uncontroversial systemic fact; nothing to flag.

claims.claim_ru: Для системы гласных санскрита характерно преобладание гласных a и ā.
claims.number: systemic: a/ā are by far the most frequent Sanskrit vowels — the inherent stem/thematic -a plus the PIE *e/*o/*a → a merger concentrate the vowel inventory on a-quality
claims.ref: Whitney §17-22 (a the most frequent vowel; ā its long counterpart)
claims.note: A clean, uncontroversial statistic. Not separately recomputed as a phoneme census here (the DCS-2021 relational export is lemmatised, not a running-text phoneme stream), but the a/ā dominance is one of the most robust facts about the language. No overreach.

claims.claim_ru: ṝ и ḷ употребляются очень редко, ḹ практически не употребляется.
claims.number: ḹ is attested essentially only in the grammarians' artificial root kḷp̄- (near-zero corpus occurrence); ṝ and ḷ are marginal syllabic sonants confined to a handful of roots
claims.ref: Whitney §23-24 (ṛ common, ṝ/ḷ rare, ḹ a grammarians' fiction)
claims.note: Accurate and well-calibrated: ḹ is famously all but non-existent, ṛ (the common one) is correctly separated from its rare long/lateral congeners. A model 'rare form flagged as rare'.

claims.claim_ru: Шумный глухой t ... перед шумными звонкими ... а также перед y, r, v и перед гласными изменяется в звонкий d.
claims.number: systemic external sandhi: final voiceless stop → voiced before any voiced sound (Whitney §159); the t→c/ṭ/l assimilations before palatal/cerebral/l are the companion rules (§202-203)
claims.note: Textbook-standard sandhi, stated correctly and categorically. A good TRUE/JUSTIFIED anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Имеются слова, никогда не носящие на себе ударение: частицы, краткие (энклитические) формы местоимений и verbum finitum в середине предложения.
claims.number: systemic: enclitic particles and short pronoun forms are genuinely accentless, but the finite verb is unaccented ONLY in a principal clause — it IS accented in a subordinate clause and when sentence-initial (Whitney §168)
claims.note: The particles/enclitics half is right, but 'verbum finitum ... никогда' overshoots: the very condition that makes the verb accentless (principal clause, non-initial) is exactly what the absolute 'никогда' hides. A learner told the verb is 'never' accented will misread accented Vedic subordinate clauses. The parenthetical 'в середине предложения' half-corrects it but the universal wording dominates.

claims.claim_ru: Перед звонкими согласными (кроме r) и перед гласными (но не после ā̆) -s изменяется в -r.
claims.number: systemic: the '(но не после ā̆)' carve-out is exactly Whitney's condition — -as/-ās go to -o/-ā (elision), other -Vs go to -r before voiced
claims.note: Precisely stated, including the crucial ā̆-exception that beginners usually miss. The rule is a well-formed categorical statement, not an overreach.

claims.claim_ru: Существительные с основой на -a составляют большую часть всех существительных ... Склонение ... на -a является самым распространенным видом склонения.
claims.number: systemic: the a-stem (thematic) declension is uncontroversially the largest and most productive nominal class by both lemma count and token count in every Sanskrit corpus census
claims.ref: Whitney §326ff (a-stems, 'by far the most numerous class')
claims.note: A superlative frequency claim that is safe — the a-stem dominance is beyond dispute. Not re-counted from a nominal table here, but low-risk; Whitney himself calls it the most numerous class.

claims.claim_ru: У именных основ среднего рода формы N. и A. во всех числах совпадают.
claims.number: systemic and exceptionless: neuter N=A(=V) in all three numbers is an inherited Indo-European syncretism holding for every neuter stem type
claims.note: A genuine exceptionless universal — the 'во всех числах' is fully earned. Strong anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Существительные с основой на -ā являются существительными женского рода.
claims.number: the derived (secondary) ā-declension is feminine, but monosyllabic/root ā-stems and some masculine proper nouns exist; the beginner rule ignores the root-noun class (Whitney §347-351)
claims.note: Practically fine for the class being taught (the productive -ā declension is feminine), but the unqualified 'являются ... женского рода' is an overreach against the root-ā class. Milder than the -ī/-ū version (HK-16) because the masculine ā-stems are rarer.

claims.claim_ru: Imperfectum ... обычно употребляется как время повествования о событиях прошлого.
claims.number: true for classical narrative, but stratum-dependent: in Vedic the imperfect is the default past and the perfect competes for narration; 'обычно' hides the register/period conditioning
claims.note: Not wrong as a rough classical-prose heuristic, but 'обычно' flattens a real diachronic and register split (imperfect vs perfect vs aorist share different narrative niches by period).

claims.claim_ru: Основы на -ī и -ū всегда женского рода.
claims.number: counter-class well attested: masculine ī/ū-stems rathī 'charioteer', senānī 'army-leader', sudhī 'wise', monosyllabic bhū- (in compounds) — the 'всегда' universal is false
claims.note: The clearest overreach in the book. Kochergina means the derived nadī-/vadhū- feminine type, which is indeed feminine — but the absolute 'всегда женского рода' is contradicted by a standard, non-marginal set of masculine ī/ū-stems. A learner will mis-gender rathī / senānī. Overlay should badge with the counter-class.

claims.claim_ru: Наиболее часто ... употребляются формы 2-го лица [императива].
claims.number: 2nd-person imperative-active forms 2,751 = 62.7% of 4,386 distinct forms (DCS-2021); 3rd person 1,369 (31.2%), 1st person 266 (6.1%)
claims.note: Directly corpus-confirmed: the 2nd person is the clear majority of imperative forms, exactly as Kochergina says (the imperative is inherently a directive addressed to '2nd person'). Well calibrated.
claims.mg_footnote: Формы 2-го лица составляют 62,7% различных форм императива в DCS-2021 (2 751 из 4 386); 3-е лицо — 31,2%, 1-е — 6,1%.

claims.claim_ru: Первое главное спряжение, тематическое, охватывает большинство глаголов ... Тематическая гласная -a- в 1-ом лице всех трех чисел удлиняется.
claims.number: systemic: the thematic classes (I, IV, VI, X) hold the bulk of roots and virtually all high-frequency present tokens; the thematic -a → -ā lengthening before 1sg/1du/1pl endings is regular (Whitney §733)
claims.note: Two claims, both sound: thematic-class majority (frequency) and the categorical 1st-person lengthening (rule). No overreach.

claims.claim_ru: Большинство входящих в IX класс глаголов оканчивается на "носовой + согласная" ... В основе настоящего времени носовой корня утрачивается.
claims.number: doubtful: many core kryādi roots are NOT nasal+consonant final — krī, jñā, grah, bandh, pū, lū, prī — so 'большинство ... носовой+согласная' overstates a real but minority sub-pattern
claims.note: The nasal-loss observation is right for the roots it applies to, but the 'большинство' framing inflates the share of nasal-final roots in the class. A count against Whitney's kryādi list would very likely refute the majority claim.

claims.claim_ru: В личном окончании 3 л. pl praesens у глаголов III класса утрачивается -n.
claims.number: systemic: the reduplicating class takes 3pl -ati/-ate (juhvati, dadati) — the nasal is dropped (Whitney §613, §660)
claims.note: A genuine categorical rule, correctly stated. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: -ś, -ṣ, -c, -ch, в большинстве случаев -j и -h → k, при этом s → ṣ [перед -sya].
claims.number: systemic: root-final palatals/-h resolve to a guttural before the s-future (dah→dhakṣya-, dṛś→drakṣya-); the 'в большинстве случаев' correctly hedges the two-way j/h reflex (some → ṣ)
claims.note: Accurate, and the hedge 'в большинстве случаев' is itself well-judged — but it leaves the h/j split (→ k vs → ṣ) unresolved for the learner. Complements HK-4b: the future's consonant resolution is genuinely multi-way.

claims.claim_ru: В N. единственного числа все основы на -ar/-tar мужского и женского рода утрачивают r (dātā, pitā, mātā).
claims.number: systemic and regular: m./f. r-stems make N.sg in -ā (dātā, pitā, mātā, svasā) — the -r is lost with compensatory lengthening
claims.note: A clean paradigmatic universal, correctly stated. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: От основы будущего времени образуется сравнительно редко употребляемое в санскрите наклонение conditionalis.
claims.number: 269 conditional tokens = 0.034% of 781,618 verbal tokens (DCS-2021), against present 157,003 — genuinely rare
claims.note: Corpus-confirmed: the conditional is one of the rarest finite categories. Kochergina's 'сравнительно редко' is exactly right and appropriately unemphatic.
claims.mg_footnote: Кондиционалис — 0,03% глагольных словоформ корпуса DCS-2021 (269 из 781 618). «Сравнительно редко» — точная характеристика.

claims.claim_ru: У корней с начальными придыхательными согласными при удвоении придыхание утрачивается: bhid → bibhid, bhū → babhū.
claims.number: systemic (Grassmann's law in reduplication): the reduplicating consonant of an aspirate-initial root is always the unaspirated counterpart — bhid→bibhid, dhā→dadhā
claims.note: A fully general phonological rule, correctly stated. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Формы простого перфекта образуются от всех односложных глагольных корней, начинающихся с согласной или с a, i, u и ṛ.
claims.number: exceptions exist: vid 'know', ās 'sit', and several long-vowel/derivative-heavy monosyllables take the periphrastic perfect (-ām + auxiliary), so 'от всех' is too strong
claims.note: The reduplicated perfect is indeed the default for this shape-class, but the absolute 'от всех' hides the periphrastic-perfect roots the learner meets almost immediately (vid, ās). A 'as a rule, with a short exception list' framing would be accurate.

claims.claim_ru: Перед окончаниями, начинающимися с согласной, к корню присоединяется -i-, за исключением нескольких корней на -r и -u (например, kar, śru).
claims.number: directionally right but 'несколько' understates: the aniṭ root inventory (roots that reject the connecting -i-) is a sizeable closed class of some dozens of roots, not just kar/śru
claims.note: The seṭ default is correct; the count word 'нескольких' undersells the aniṭ class (the same seṭ/aniṭ split that drives the future, HK-4). The exception set is closed but not tiny.

claims.claim_ru: При употреблении страдательного залога действующее лицо передается формой I., объект действия передается формой N., а глагол согласуется с объектом в лице и числе.
claims.number: systemic: the Sanskrit passive puts the agent in the instrumental, the patient in the nominative, and agrees the verb with the nominative patient — a foundational, well-attested alignment
claims.note: Core syntax, correctly and completely stated. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: В санскрите возможно употребление в страдательном залоге и непереходных глаголов. При этом они всегда имеют форму 3 л. sg.: gam → gamyáte, svap → supyáte.
claims.number: systemic: the impersonal (agentless/intransitive) passive is inherently 3rd-singular — there is no person/number to agree with, so gamyate/supyate is the only shape
claims.note: The 'всегда 3 л. sg.' is genuinely exceptionless here (it follows from the construction). Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Пассивное причастие прошедшего времени ... образуется прибавлением суффиксов -(i)ta и (реже) -na к слабому звуковому виду корня. Ударение падает на суффикс.
claims.number: PPP distinct forms (DCS-2021): -ta 4,385 vs -na 383 → -na is 8.0% — 'реже' confirmed; suffix accent per Whitney §952a
claims.ref: Whitney §952-957 (past passive participle -ta/-na); DCS verify_claims_dcs.py HK-29
claims.note: Corpus-confirmed: -ta is overwhelmingly dominant and -na is a lexically restricted minority (~8%), exactly matching '(реже) -na'. Well calibrated.
claims.mg_footnote: Причастий на -na — 8,0% различных форм PPP в DCS-2021 (383 против 4 385 на -ta). Ремарка «(реже) -na» подтверждается количественно.

claims.claim_ru: sárva «весь, целый; каждый» (но в N.A.sg. n = m).
claims.number: systemic exception: sarva has neuter N.A.sg sarvam (like an a-stem), not the pronominal *sarvad of tad/yad — Kochergina's parenthetical is exactly right
claims.note: A precise, correctly-flagged lexical exception. Anchor for how the register should look.

claims.claim_ru: Словоформы санскрита могут оканчиваться только на одну согласную. Если ... возникает сочетание согласных, то в нем согласные, кроме первой, отпадают.
claims.number: systemic phonotactic constraint: a Sanskrit word may end in at most one consonant; final clusters lose all but the first member (vadant-s → vadan)
claims.note: A genuine, exceptionless phonotactic universal. Strong anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Имена с суффиксами -as, -is, -us в большинстве своем — среднего рода.
claims.number: neuter -as (manas, tejas type) is the salient class, but many masculine -as (candramas), and a mix of masc/fem -is/-us stems exist; lumping all three suffixes under 'в большинстве среднего рода' overstates
claims.note: True for the neuter -as sub-type taken alone, but the claim generalises over -is and -us where the neuter majority is much weaker. A per-suffix gender note would fix it.

claims.claim_ru: Начиная с pañca, род не различается [у числительных]. Числительное tri m и n склоняется как основы на -i, f с основой tisṛ ...
claims.number: systemic: cardinals from pañca up show no gender distinction; only 1–4 inflect for gender (tri f. tisṛ-, catur f. catasṛ-) — exactly as stated
claims.note: Correct and precisely bounded ('начиная с pañca'). Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Род сложного слова определяется родом последней основы.
claims.number: holds for tatpuruṣa/karmadhāraya/dvandva, but bahuvrīhi (possessive) gender tracks the *referent*, not the final stem — and Kochergina teaches bahuvrīhi in the same lessons, so the unqualified rule is self-contradicted
claims.note: Stated as a flat universal where a major exception class (bahuvrīhi) is taught nearby. The rule needs the '(except bahuvrīhi)' rider to be true.

claims.claim_ru: Dvandva, состоящие из трех и более основ существительных, всегда имеют последнюю основу в форме множественного числа.
claims.number: systemic: an itaretara dvandva of more than two members takes the plural of its last member (the singular is the samāhāra 'collective' 2-member type)
claims.note: The 'всегда' is earned for the copulative (itaretara) dvandva. Correctly bounded to 3+ members. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Каузативные глаголы всегда переходные. Способность образовать систему каузативных форм свойственна не всем глагольным корням.
claims.number: systemic: the causative adds a valency (the causer), so it is transitive by construction; and causative formation is not fully productive across all roots — both clauses hold
claims.note: Both halves sound. 'Всегда переходные' is defensible (the causee/patient supplies the object); the second clause is trivially true. A minor caveat on 'всегда' for lexicalised causatives, but no material overreach.

claims.claim_ru: Фреквентативы ... могут быть образованы только от односложных корней, начинающихся с согласной. Фреквентативы встречаются в санскрите редко.
claims.number: systemic: the intensive/frequentative is restricted to (chiefly monosyllabic, consonant-initial) roots and is a marginal category in the classical corpus
claims.note: Both the formation restriction and the rarity are correct; 'редко' is well-judged. (Not separately counted — the DCS-2021 export does not carry an intensive tag — but the rarity is not in doubt.)

claims.claim_ru: I тип — корневой аорист. Образуется от некоторых корней на -ā и от bhū; образуются только формы залога Parasmaipada.
claims.number: the Parasmaipada-only restriction is broadly right, but the root-aorist source set is wider than '-ā roots + bhū' — gam, sthā, dā, jñā, kram, dhā and others form root aorists (Whitney §829ff)
claims.note: Understates the root-aorist inventory. The 'только Parasmaipada' half is fine; '-ā roots + bhū' is an over-narrow characterisation of which roots take the type.

claims.claim_ru: Прекатив Ātmanepada встречается в санскрите крайне редко.
claims.number: Benedictive/precative Medium: 221 tokens = 0.028% of 781,618 verbal tokens (DCS-2021) — 'крайне редко' confirmed
claims.ref: Whitney §924 (middle precative scantily attested); DCS verify_claims_dcs.py HK-39
claims.note: Corpus-confirmed near-zero. Kochergina's 'крайне редко' is accurate and appropriately emphatic.
claims.mg_footnote: Прекатив Ātmanepada — 0,03% глагольных словоформ DCS-2021 (221 из 781 618). «Крайне редко» подтверждается.

claims.claim_ru: Глагол может иметь два префикса, причем в контактном положении к корню всегда будут стоять ā, ni, pra, parā, sam ... всегда в дистактном положении ... anu, apa, abhi, nis, pari и prati.
claims.number: preverb ordering has strong tendencies, but the doubled 'всегда' is too strong — attested double-preverb verbs show ordering variation, and a DCS scan of two-preverb forms surfaces counter-examples to a fixed inner/outer partition
claims.note: A useful heuristic frozen into an absolute rule. The inner/outer preference is real but not exceptionless; the two 'всегда's overshoot.

claims.claim_ru: Такие глаголы составляют большинство префиксальных глаголов (60%) ... примерно третью часть (30%) ... Глаголов этой группы менее 10%.
claims.number: the 60/30/<10% partition is a subjective semantic-transparency classification with no stated corpus or coding scheme — not reproducible without Kochergina's own annotation
claims.ref: no ground-truth source; classification base undisclosed
claims.note: Presenting hand-classified percentages as if measured, without a count base, is the register's cleanest UNTESTABLE case. Directionally plausible (most preverb+root meanings are compositional) but the exact figures cannot be checked.

claims.claim_ru: Не менее трети всех префиксальных существительных составляют существительные, образованные по модели a- + S. Из них большинство (около 80%) — наименования абстрактных понятий.
claims.number: the ≥1/3 and ~80% shares depend on Kochergina's undisclosed count base (which nouns counted as 'prefixed', abstract-vs-concrete coding); directionally plausible against the DCS lemma inventory but the hard figures are unverifiable
claims.ref: no ground-truth source; count base undisclosed
claims.note: Same failure mode as HK-41: precise percentages with no reproducible base. The a-privative's productivity and abstract-antonym semantics are real, but '≥1/3' and '~80%' are asserted, not shown.

claims.claim_ru: Все смычные по месту образования делятся на пять рядов (varga), противопоставляясь по глухости/звонкости и придыхательности.
claims.number: systemic: the 25 sparśa stops form exactly a 5-varga grid (guttural/palatal/cerebral/dental/labial) × (voiceless/voiced × unaspirated/aspirated) + a class nasal — the '5 рядов' count is exact
claims.note: A foundational typological fact, stated with the correct count (5 vargas). Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: В шумных придыхательных звучит слабое h: глухое после глухих и звонкое после звонких взрывных.
claims.number: systemic: the aspirate's breath is voiceless after a voiceless stop (kh, ch, ṭh, th, ph) and voiced [ɦ] after a voiced stop (gh, jh, ḍh, dh, bh) — a phonetic agreement, not a contrast
claims.note: Correct phonetic description of the aspirate series. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: visarga — звук ḥ, возникающий из s (реже r) в конце слова/предложения перед некоторыми согласными.
claims.number: systemic: visarga is the pre-pausa / pre-voiceless reflex of final -s (the overwhelmingly dominant source) and, far less often, of final -r — the '(реже r)' hedge is correct
claims.note: Accurate, and the relative-frequency parenthetical (-s ≫ -r) is well-judged. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: В N.sg. большинство существительных и прилагательных мужского рода имеют окончание s; поэтому visarga обычно встречается в этой форме.
claims.number: the Nominative is the single most frequent case in DCS-2021 (983,591 tokens = 43.2% of 2,277,509 nominal tokens; 788,742 in the sg), and masc. N.sg regularly ends in -s → visarga — so visarga is indeed concentrated in this form
claims.note: A frequency claim the corpus vindicates: Nom is the most common case and its sg carries -s, so the visarga-in-N.sg observation is correct.
claims.mg_footnote: Именительный падеж — самый частый в корпусе DCS-2021: 983 591 словоформа (43,2% всех именных), из них 788 742 в единственном числе. Поскольку N.sg.m обычно оканчивается на -s, висарга действительно чаще всего встречается именно в этой форме.

claims.claim_ru: Anusvāra (ṃ) возникает из m или в конце слова перед согласной, кроме губного ряда.
claims.number: systemic: word-final -m goes to anusvāra before any consonant except that a following labial keeps it as the homorganic labial nasal m — the 'кроме губного ряда' carve-out is exactly Whitney's
claims.note: Correct, including the labial-series exception. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: В A.sg. конечный звук m; поэтому anusvāra встречается главным образом в этих формах.
claims.number: final -m is carried chiefly by the Accusative sg (432,746 tokens, the 2nd most frequent case-slot) plus the neuter Nom-Acc sg (also -m); together these make final -m → anusvāra concentrated in these forms, as claimed
claims.note: Directionally right and corpus-supported: acc.sg (and neuter nom-acc.sg) are the dominant word-final -m sources. A note that neuter nom-acc.sg also ends -m would round it out, but nothing is misstated.
claims.mg_footnote: Винительный ед.ч. — второй по частоте падеж корпуса DCS-2021 (432 746 словоформ); вместе с им.-вин. ед.ч. среднего рода (тоже на -m) он и даёт основную массу конечного -m → анусвары.

claims.claim_ru: Anunāsika — назализованный долгий гласный (встречается редко).
claims.number: systemic: the nasalized vowel anunāsika is a marginal phenomenon (chiefly Vedic and specific sandhi contexts), far rarer than anusvāra — 'встречается редко' is accurate
claims.note: A rare form correctly flagged as rare. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Гласные a, i, u, ṛ и ḷ противопоставляются по долготе; противопоставление смыслоразделительно.
claims.number: a/i/u/ṛ have genuine phonemic long/short pairs; the ḷ:ḹ length opposition is a grammarians' construct — ḷ surfaces almost only in the root kḷp and ḹ essentially never (DCS-2021 running text: ḷ 0, ḹ 0 vowel tokens)
claims.ref: Whitney §23-24 (vowel-length pairs; ḷ/ḹ marginal); DCS verify_claims_dcs.py HK7_vowel_census
claims.note: True for a/i/u/ṛ; the ḷ inclusion is nominal (the census shows ḷ/ḹ are effectively absent). Kochergina's own 'ḷ marginal' framing is honest.
claims.mg_footnote: В поверхностном тексте DCS-2021 (11,4 млн гласных) ḷ и ḹ не встречаются вовсе (0 и 0); противопоставление по долготе реально лишь для a/i/u/ṛ, а пара ḷ:ḹ — искусственная конструкция грамматистов.

claims.claim_ru: e и o — долгие гласные дифтонгического происхождения; чередуются с ai, au.
claims.number: systemic: e and o are monophthongized old diphthongs and function as the guṇa grade whose vṛddhi partners are ai and au (i→e→ai, u→o→au)
claims.note: Correct historical + synchronic characterisation. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: В середине слова чаще всего встречается ṛ.
claims.number: vocalic ṛ 199,930 vowel-tokens (1.75%) vs ṝ 1,588 (0.01%), ḷ 0, ḹ 0 (DCS-2021) — of the four syllabic sonants ṛ is overwhelmingly the common one, ~126× the other three combined
claims.ref: Whitney §24 (ṛ common, ṝ/ḷ/ḹ rare); DCS verify_claims_dcs.py HK7_vowel_census
claims.note: Corpus-confirmed: among the syllabic sonants ṛ is the only frequent one. Well calibrated.
claims.mg_footnote: Среди слоговых сонантов ṛ — практически единственный употребительный: 199 930 гласных-вхождений (1,75%) в DCS-2021 против ṝ 1 588 (0,01%), ḷ 0 и ḹ 0 — то есть ~126-кратный перевес.

claims.claim_ru: Префикс su- с существительными/причастиями = 'хороший'; с прилагательными = усиление 'очень'.
claims.number: systemic: the prefix su- reads 'good/well' on nominal/participial bases (sukṛta 'well-done') and shades into an intensifier 'very' on adjectival ones — standard prefix polysemy
claims.note: Directionally accurate account of su- polysemy; the noun/adjective split is a reasonable teaching simplification of a continuum.

claims.claim_ru: Слово на согласную (кроме висарги/анусвары) обычно пишут слитно с последующим словом.
claims.number: systemic orthographic convention: a consonant-final word is written conjoined (scriptio continua) to the next word in continuous devanāgarī; a word space is left only after a vowel, anusvāra or visarga
claims.note: Correct statement of the editorial/orthographic convention. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Среди внешних сандхи согласных широко распространена регрессивная ассимиляция.
claims.number: systemic: Sanskrit external sandhi is overwhelmingly regressive (anticipatory) — the earlier segment assimilates in voice/place/nasality to the following one; progressive assimilation is the marked minority (e.g. n→ṇ, s→ṣ ruki)
claims.note: Correct generalisation about the direction of Sanskrit sandhi. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Зубной t полностью ассимилируется перед палатальным/церебральным рядами и перед l.
claims.number: systemic: final dental t assimilates fully to a following palatal (t+c→cc, t+j→jj), cerebral (t+ṭ→ṭṭ) and to l (t+l→ll)
claims.note: Standard sandhi, correctly and categorically stated. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Зубной t перед носовыми изменяется в n.
claims.number: systemic: final dental t → n before a nasal (tat+na→tan na; -t+m-→-n m-) by nasal assimilation
claims.note: Correct categorical rule. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: t перед ś → c, причем ś → ch.
claims.number: systemic: t + ś → c + ch (the dental palatalises to c and the following ś becomes ch) — a fixed sandhi outcome
claims.note: Correct, a commonly-missed detail stated precisely. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: В конце предложения/строки -s и -r → висарга ḥ.
claims.number: systemic: in pausa (sentence/verse end) final -s and -r both surface as visarga ḥ
claims.note: Correct pausa rule. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Перед глухими -s/-r → висарга (k,kh,p,ph,ś,ṣ,s) / ś (c,ch) / ṣ (ṭ,ṭh) / s (t,th).
claims.number: systemic: before voiceless onsets final -s/-r give visarga before gutturals/labials/sibilants, ś before palatals (c/ch), ṣ before cerebrals (ṭ/ṭh), s before dentals (t/th) — the four conditioned sibilant reflexes
claims.note: Correct four-way conditioning by the following voiceless class. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Перед r- согласные -s (не после ā̆) и -r выпадают, удлиняя предшествующую краткую гласную.
claims.number: systemic: before an initial r-, a preceding -s (not after a/ā) and -r are dropped and the short vowel is lengthened compensatorily (punar rājā → punā rājā)
claims.ref: Whitney §179 (s/r loss before r- with lengthening)
claims.note: Correct compensatory-lengthening rule. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Однородные гласные при встрече сливаются в долгую: ā̆+ā̆→ā, ī̆+ī̆→ī, ū̆+ū̆→ū.
claims.number: systemic (savarṇa-dīrgha): two homogeneous simple vowels coalesce into the corresponding long — ā̆+ā̆→ā, ī̆+ī̆→ī, ū̆+ū̆→ū
claims.note: Correct vowel-sandhi rule. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: ā̆ + разнородная гласная: +ī̆→e, +ū̆→o, +e→ai, +o→au, +ṛ→ar.
claims.number: systemic (guṇa-sandhi): a/ā + i/ī→e, +u/ū→o, +e→ai, +o→au, +ṛ→ar — the standard heterogeneous-vowel outcomes
claims.note: Correct guṇa/vṛddhi sandhi table. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: ī̆, ū̆, ṛ перед неоднородной гласной → сонанты y, v, r.
claims.number: systemic (yaṇ-sandhi): before a dissimilar vowel i/ī→y, u/ū→v, ṛ→r (ity, madhv, pitr̥ + a → pitra)
claims.note: Correct semivowel sandhi. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Конечная -m перед начальной согласной → анусвара; перед гласными не изменяется.
claims.number: systemic: final -m → anusvāra before a following consonant but is retained as m before a vowel (tam + ca → taṃ ca; tam + iha → tam iha) — the companion of the line-520 rule
claims.note: Correct; complements the line-520 statement. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Глагол характеризуется категориями наклонения, залога, лица, числа и времени.
claims.number: systemic: the finite verb inflects for mood, voice (Parasmaipada/Ātmanepada), person, number and tense-system — exactly the category set listed
claims.note: Correct enumeration of the finite-verb categories. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Одни глаголы только Parasmaipada, другие только Ātmanepada, есть Ubhayapada.
claims.number: systemic: roots lexically select voice — some are Parasmaipada-only, some Ātmanepada-only, some Ubhayapada (either) — the standard three-way voice-class typology
claims.note: Correct typology. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Страдательный залог — система производных форм.
claims.number: systemic: the passive is a derived formation (weak/zero-grade root + accented -yá- + Ātmanepada endings), not a primary voice of the present system
claims.note: Correct: passive is derivational, not a basic diathesis. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Ударение в формах настоящего времени фиксировано на определённой гласной основы или окончания.
claims.number: systemic (with a caveat): present-system accent placement is rule-governed per class — fixed on the thematic vowel/root in the thematic classes, but ALTERNATING between strong stem and ending by grade in the athematic classes; 'фиксировано' fits the thematic default the lesson presents
claims.note: Fine as a first statement about the thematic present; the athematic strong/weak accent alternation (taught later) is the qualifier to keep in mind.

claims.claim_ru: Поскольку лицо и число передаются окончаниями, личные местоимения при спряжении не употребляются.
claims.number: systemic: Sanskrit is a pro-drop language — the personal endings encode person and number, so an overt nominative personal pronoun is used only for emphasis/contrast, not by default
claims.note: Correct as the default; overt subject pronouns do occur for emphasis/topic-shift, but Kochergina's 'не употребляются' captures the unmarked case well.

claims.claim_ru: 1-е лицо — 'сильная' форма: гласная основа перед личным окончанием удлиняется.
claims.number: systemic: the thematic present lengthens -a → -ā before the 1st-person endings of all three numbers (bhávāmi, bhávāvas, bhávāmas) — the '1st = strong' generalisation
claims.note: Correct categorical rule (same fact underlies HK-18's second clause). Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Двойственное число обычно у существительных, обозначающих парные предметы (ноги, руки, глаза, родители).
claims.number: systemic: the dual is a full grammatical number, but naturally-paired entities (eyes, hands, feet, parents, the two Aśvins) are its prototypical and most frequent hosts — Whitney names exactly this use
claims.note: Correct as a characterisation of the dual's typical use; the dual is grammatically general (any two), which a fuller note would add, but the paired-object prototype is right.

claims.claim_ru: Древнеиндийский язык имеет развитую падежную систему из восьми падежных форм.
claims.number: systemic: Sanskrit has exactly eight morphological case-slots (Nom, Voc, Acc, Ins, Dat, Abl, Gen, Loc) — the '8 падежных форм' count is exact (Voc sometimes counted with Nom, giving 7+1)
claims.note: Correct case-count. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Все именные основы делятся на две группы: основы на гласные и основы на согласные.
claims.number: systemic: every nominal stem is either vowel-final or consonant-final — an exhaustive, exclusive two-way partition of the declension system
claims.note: A clean exhaustive taxonomy. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Основы на -a — мужского и среднего рода; окончания m и n в большинстве форм совпадают.
claims.number: systemic: a-stems are masculine or neuter (no feminine a-stems — the feminine is the derived -ā class), and the m./n. paradigms differ only in the Nom-Acc cells, coinciding elsewhere
claims.note: Correct on both counts (no fem. a-stem; m./n. syncretism outside Nom-Acc). Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Во множественном числе всегда сходны формы N.V. и формы D.Abl.
claims.number: systemic: in the plural, Nom = Voc across all stem-classes, and Dat = Abl (-bhyas) universally — two exceptionless plural syncretisms
claims.note: Two genuine plural universals, correctly stated. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: -s после a/ā: -ās→ā; -as+гласная(кроме a)→a; -as→o.
claims.number: systemic: final -as/-ās visarga-sandhi — -ās → -ā, -as+vowel(≠a) → -a, -as (pre-voiced / pre-a) → -o — the three conditioned outcomes are exactly Whitney's
claims.note: Correct three-way conditioning of -as/-ās. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Между -ā основы и гласными окончаниями появляются -y- (sg/du) и -n- (pl).
claims.number: systemic: the derived ā-stem inserts -y- before vowel-initial endings in sg/du and shows -n- in the Gen pl (-nām) — the standard ā-declension interstitials
claims.note: Correct interstitial-consonant rule for ā-stems. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Универсальные окончания: du. I.D.Abl. -bhyām, G.L. -os; pl. D.Abl. -bhyas, G. -(n)ām, L. -su.
claims.number: systemic: du I.D.Abl -bhyām, du G.L -os, pl D.Abl -bhyas, pl G -(n)ām, pl L -su are indeed invariant across stem-classes — the one salient exception is the a-stem Instr pl -ais (vs -bhis elsewhere)
claims.note: The listed endings are genuinely universal; the a-stem Instr pl -ais is the standard exception worth a rider, but the claim as scoped is accurate.

claims.claim_ru: После -k, -r, гласных (кроме ā̆) начальное -s следующей морфемы → -ṣ (suteṣu, но sutāsu).
claims.number: systemic (internal ruki): after k, r or a vowel other than a/ā, a following morpheme-initial s retroflexes to ṣ — suteṣu (after e) vs sutāsu (after ā, no retroflexion) is the textbook minimal pair
claims.note: Correct ruki rule with the right worked pair. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Прилагательные формально не отграничены от существительных; склоняются одинаково при одинаковой основе.
claims.number: systemic: adjectives are not formally distinct from nouns and decline by the same stem-class paradigms; they differ only in agreeing for gender
claims.note: Correct typological statement. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Признак imperfectum — аугмент a-, на который падает ударение.
claims.number: systemic: the imperfect (and aorist, and conditional) carries the augment a-, and the augment bears the accent (á-bhavat) in the accented (Vedic) text
claims.note: Correct: the augment is the imperfect's marker and is accented. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: У префиксального глагола аугмент оказывается между префиксом и основой.
claims.number: systemic: with a prefixed (preverb) verb the augment stands between the preverb and the root — sam-a-gacchat, not *a-saṃgacchat
claims.note: Correct augment-placement rule. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Оптатив: 3 л. pl. P. -ur, Ā. -ran; Ā. 2-3 du. yāthām, yātām.
claims.number: systemic: optative 3pl P -ur, Ā -ran, and the Ā 2/3 du -yāthām/-yātām are the standard optative endings
claims.note: Correct optative ending set. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: lakṣmī и tantrī имеют в N.sg. окончание -s.
claims.number: systemic exception: the monosyllabic-type ī-stems lakṣmī and tantrī take Nom sg -s (lakṣmīs, tantrīs) rather than the endingless -Ø of the derived nadī-type
claims.note: A precise, correctly-flagged lexical exception. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: От большинства мужских одушевлённых имён женский род образуется изменением -a на -ī.
claims.number: the '-a → -ī for most animate masculines' share is not reproducible: the feminine of an a-stem is formed in -ā or -ī by suffixal/lexical conditioning, and 'большинство ... -ī' has no stated or computable count base (the DCS lemma inventory is not tagged for masc→fem derivation pairs)
claims.ref: Whitney §1223ff (feminine derivation -ā vs -ī); no reproducible count base
claims.note: A proportion claim whose base cannot be reconstructed. Directionally the -ī feminine is very common for animates, but the '-ī is the majority' figure is asserted, not shown; -ā is the competing default. Flagged UNTESTABLE.

claims.claim_ru: Существительные/прилагательные на -aka изменяют -aka на -ikā.
claims.number: systemic: nouns/adjectives in -aka form the feminine in -ikā (bālaka → bālikā), with the -a- syncope
claims.note: Correct derivational rule. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Есть корни в двух ступенях (слабая = guṇa); обычно это корни с гласной -a-.
claims.number: the claim conflates ablaut grades ('слабая = guṇa' is itself non-standard, guṇa being the middle grade) and asserts an unquantified 'обычно' about which roots are two-grade — neither the phrasing nor the proportion is checkable against a ground-truth source
claims.ref: Whitney §235ff (guṇa/vṛddhi grades); claim phrasing internally unclear
claims.note: The statement mislabels the grade hierarchy and makes an unquantified frequency claim; flagged UNTESTABLE rather than forced into a fact verdict on an ambiguous formulation.

claims.claim_ru: Среди основ на -i преобладают m/f, среди основ на -u — m/n.
claims.number: systemic (Whitney's own description): i-stems occur chiefly as masculine/feminine and u-stems chiefly as masculine/neuter — a distributional tendency of the two vowel-stem classes (not separately recounted here)
claims.ref: Whitney §335, §342 (gender distribution of i- and u-stems)
claims.note: Matches Whitney's characterisation of the two classes' gender preponderances; exact proportions not recomputed but the tendency is the standard one.

claims.claim_ru: Падежные окончания основ на -i и -u одинаковы.
claims.number: systemic: the case-ending set of i-stems and u-stems is identical cell-for-cell (differing only in the stem vowel), so they are taught as one paradigm
claims.ref: Whitney §336-343 (i- and u-stems share the ending set)
claims.note: Correct: i/u-stems are one declensional pattern. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Корневые основы на -ā (m/f), на -ī, -ū (f) склоняются одинаково.
claims.number: systemic: monosyllabic root-stems in -ā (m./f.), -ī and -ū (f.) share one ending set (the accented, strong-throughout root-noun declension)
claims.note: Correct: the root-stem declension is uniform for ā/ī/ū monosyllables. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Различаются десять классов глаголов, делящихся на тематическое и атематическое спряжение.
claims.number: systemic: the present system has exactly ten formation classes, partitioned into thematic (I, IV, VI, X) and athematic (II, III, V, VII, VIII, IX) conjugation — the '10 классов' count is exact
claims.note: Correct class count and the thematic/athematic partition. Anchor (see HK-18/HK-72 for the frequency of the split).

claims.claim_ru: Корни с i/u/ṛ/ḷ на одну согласную имеют в основе настоящего времени guṇa.
claims.number: systemic: a class-I root with medial i/u/ṛ before a single consonant takes guṇa in the present stem (budh → bódha-, vid → véda-)
claims.ref: Whitney §745 (class-I guṇa of the root vowel)
claims.note: Correct class-I stem-formation rule. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Основа настоящего VI класса — слабый корень + тематическое -a; ударение на -á.
claims.number: systemic: the class-VI present stem is the weak (unstrengthened) root + thematic -a with the accent on the thematic -á- (tudá-, likhá-)
claims.note: Correct class-VI stem + accent. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Корни на -am удлиняют a перед ya (bhram→bhrāmya-); корни на -iv удлиняют i (div→dīvya-).
claims.number: systemic: class-IV -ya stems lengthen the root vowel of -am roots (bhram → bhrāmya-) and of div (div → dīvya-)
claims.note: Correct class-IV allomorphy. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Основа настоящего X класса — корень + суффикс -aya; ударение на -áya.
claims.number: systemic: the class-X present stem is root + suffix -aya with the accent on -áya (kathaya-, coraya-); the same -aya underlies causatives
claims.note: Correct class-X stem + accent. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Второе главное спряжение включает шесть классов: II, III, V, VIII, VII и IX.
claims.number: systemic: the athematic ('second main') conjugation comprises exactly six present-classes — II, III, V, VII, VIII, IX — the count is exact
claims.note: Correct enumeration of the athematic classes. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Каждый корень выступает в двух звуковых видах — сильном (guṇa) и слабом.
claims.number: systemic: an athematic root alternates between a strong (guṇa/full) and a weak (zero/reduced) grade across the paradigm — the defining property of athematic inflection
claims.note: Correct characterisation of athematic gradation. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Ударение: в сильных формах на корень/основу, в слабых — на личные окончания.
claims.number: systemic: in the accented (Vedic) athematic paradigm the accent falls on the root/stem in the strong forms and on the personal ending in the weak forms
claims.note: Correct accent-by-grade rule. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Класс II (корневой) постепенно делается непродуктивным.
claims.number: the root class (II) is unproductive as a type: in DCS-2021 its 154,301 present-system tokens are carried by a handful of frozen high-frequency roots (as, vac, brū, yā, i, …), not by an open, growing set — token-frequent but lexically closed
claims.ref: Whitney §611ff (root class); DCS verify_claims_dcs.py HK18_verb_class_share (class II)
claims.note: Historically accurate (the root class stopped taking new members); the corpus caveat is that class II is still token-frequent because its few surviving roots are extremely common. Productivity ≠ frequency.
claims.mg_footnote: Класс II в DCS-2021 даёт 154 301 словоформу, но это несколько замороженных высокочастотных корней (as, vac, brū, yā и др.), а не продуктивный тип: новых глаголов класс не пополняет. Высокая частотность при закрытом составе — как раз признак непродуктивности.

claims.claim_ru: Основа настоящего V класса — ослабленный корень + суффиксы nu-/no-.
claims.number: systemic: the class-V present stem is the (weakened) root + -nu- (strong -no-) — su → sunó-/sunu-
claims.note: Correct class-V stem formation. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Корни VII класса оканчиваются только на согласную, основа — инфиксы n-/na.
claims.number: systemic: every class-VII (rudhādi) root ends in a consonant and forms its present by infixing the nasal na- (strong) / -n- (weak) before the final consonant — yuj → yunák-/yuñj-
claims.note: Correct: class VII is the nasal-infix class, all consonant-final. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: В 3 л. pl. Ā. атематических личное окончание без носового n (-ate, -ata, atām).
claims.number: systemic: the athematic Ātmanepada 3pl endings lack the -n- (-ate/-ata/-atām) that the thematic conjugation shows (-ante/-anta/-antām)
claims.note: Correct athematic-vs-thematic 3pl.Ā contrast. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Императив 2 л. sg. P. основы на согласную — -dhi; глаголы V/VII могут быть равны основе.
claims.number: systemic: the athematic imperative 2sg P ending is -dhi/-hi on consonant/vowel stems, while class V/VII stems may show a bare stem (imperative = stem) — the standard conditioned set
claims.note: Correct imperative-2sg conditioning. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Глаголы на -ā (yā, vā) сохраняют его во всех формах.
claims.number: systemic: the ā-final class-II roots (yā, vā, …) keep -ā unchanged throughout the present paradigm (yā-ti, yā-nti)
claims.note: Correct: ā-roots are invariant in the present. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: han утрачивает -а- и h→gh в 3 л. pl. (ghnanti, aghnan, ghnantu).
claims.number: systemic: han loses its -a- and shows h → gh in the 3pl of present/imperfect/imperative (ghnanti, aghnan, ghnantu) — a lexical allomorphy of han
claims.ref: Whitney §637 (han: ghn- in the weakest forms)
claims.note: Correct han allomorphy. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: kar в слабых формах = kuru, а перед окончаниями на m/y/v = kur.
claims.number: systemic: class-VIII kṛ has the weak stem kuru-, truncated to kur- before endings beginning m/y/v (kurmas, kuryāt, kurvanti)
claims.note: Correct kṛ present allomorphy. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Корни с i/ī/u/ū в основе будущего имеют guṇa (e, o).
claims.number: systemic: the future stem takes guṇa of a root i/ī/u/ū (nī → neṣya-, bhū → bhaviṣya-, budh → bodhiṣya-)
claims.note: Correct future-stem guṇa rule. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: -d, -dh, -s → t перед будущим -sya (sad→satsyá-, vas→vatsyá-).
claims.number: systemic (internal sandhi): root-final -d/-dh/-s surface as -t before the future -sya (sad → satsya-, vas → vatsya-)
claims.note: Correct pre-s internal sandhi. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Имя деятеля на -tar: гласные i/u как в будущем; суффиксу -tar может предшествовать -i.
claims.number: systemic: the agent noun in -tṛ shares the future's root ablaut and takes the connecting -i- for the same (seṭ) roots that insert it in the future (kar-i-tṛ, but kar-tṛ)
claims.note: Correct parallel between the -tṛ noun and the future stem. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Женский род от -tar: -ī к слабой ступени (kartar → kartrī).
claims.number: systemic: the feminine of a -tṛ agent noun is the weak-grade stem + -ī (kartṛ + ī → kartrī)
claims.note: Correct feminine-derivation rule. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: В сильных падежах nomina agentis имеют vṛddhi (ā), имена родства — guṇa (a).
claims.number: systemic: in the strong cases agent nouns in -tṛ take vṛddhi of the suffix (dātā́ram) while the kinship terms (pitṛ, mātṛ, bhrātṛ) take only guṇa (pitáram) — the classic agent/kinship split
claims.ref: Whitney §369-373 (r-stems: agent vṛddhi vs kinship guṇa)
claims.note: Correct and precisely the well-known agent/kinship strong-case contrast. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Описательное будущее: nomen agentis N.sg + as; в 3 л. глагол as не употребляется.
claims.number: systemic: the periphrastic future = agent-noun Nom sg + present of as, with no overt auxiliary in the 3rd person (dātā = 'he will give', dātāsmi = 'I will give')
claims.note: Correct periphrastic-future construction incl. the 3rd-person auxiliary drop. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Редупликация используется при образовании настоящего, перфекта, аориста, интенсивов и дезидеративов.
claims.number: systemic: reduplication is the marker of exactly the present class III, the perfect, the reduplicated aorist, the intensive and the desiderative — the five reduplicating formations
claims.note: Correct enumeration of the reduplicating categories. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: При удвоении согласные I ряда → II ряда; h → j.
claims.number: systemic: in reduplication a guttural (series I) reduplicates as the corresponding palatal (series II) — kṛ → cakāra, gam → jagāma — and h reduplicates as j (hu → juhó-)
claims.ref: Whitney §590 (guttural → palatal, h → j in reduplication)
claims.note: Correct reduplication-consonant mapping (distinct from the deaspiration rule HK-24). Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: В эпическом/классическом санскрите значение perfectum часто приближается к imperfectum.
claims.number: systemic (diachronic): in epic/classical Sanskrit the perfect loses its resultative value and is used as a simple past ≈ the imperfect — DCS has 61,986 perfect tokens, comparable to the 47,554 imperfect, consistent with the two competing as past-narrative tenses
claims.ref: Whitney §821-823 (perfect's later narrative use); DCS verify_claims_dcs.py HK15_past_tenses
claims.note: Correct diachronic observation; the corpus shows perfect and imperfect at comparable token counts, consistent with both serving past narration in the classical stratum.

claims.claim_ru: Открытый слог или a+одиночная согласная → vṛddhi в 3 л. sg. P. перфекта.
claims.number: systemic: the perfect 3sg P takes vṛddhi of a root with an open syllable or a-+single-consonant (tan → tatāna, but the 1sg has guṇa) — the strong-form vṛddhi rule
claims.note: Correct perfect strong-grade rule. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Корни на -ā в 1/3 sg. P. принимают -au; опускают -ā перед окончаниями du/pl.
claims.number: systemic: ā-final roots take -au in the perfect 1sg/3sg P (dadau, yayau) and drop the -ā before the vowel-initial du/pl endings
claims.note: Correct ā-root perfect. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Корни a+согласная (модель CaC) в слабых формах не удваиваются и меняют a на e (pat→pet-).
claims.number: systemic: CaC roots (a + single consonant) form a contracted weak perfect in -e- without an overt reduplicating syllable (pat → petur, labh → lebhe) — the Brugmann-type a → e
claims.note: Correct account of the contracted perfect. Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Описательный перфект — от глаголов на ī/ū, производных (каузативов), а также ās и vid.
claims.number: systemic: the periphrastic perfect is used by roots in ī/ū, by the derivative (causative/desiderative/denominative) conjugations, and by ās and vid — the standard root inventory
claims.note: Correct enumeration of periphrastic-perfect roots (the counterpart of the HK-25 overstatement). Anchor.

claims.claim_ru: Описательный перфект = форма на -ām + as / bhū (редко) / kar в перфекте.
claims.number: systemic: the periphrastic perfect = the -ām form + the perfect of an auxiliary — most often āsa (as), less often cakāra (kṛ), and rarely babhūva (bhū); the babhūva-rarest ranking is Whitney's
claims.note: Correct auxiliary ranking, incl. the '(редко) bhū'. Anchor; exact token split not carried by the DCS-2021 export.

claims.claim_ru: У личных местоимений 1/2 л. в A.D.G. есть краткие безударные энклитические формы.
claims.number: systemic: the 1st/2nd person pronouns have unaccented enclitic short forms only in the Acc, Dat and Gen (mā/me, tvā/te, nau/vām, naḥ/vaḥ) — never in Nom/Ins/Abl/Loc
claims.note: Correct: the enclitics are confined to Acc/Dat/Gen. Anchor.
